title: Groin Bulge - Inguinal Hernia
chief_complaint: Right groin bulge for 3 months, increases with coughing and standing, reduces when lying down. Occasional discomfort. No pain currently.

physical_exam.groin: Reducible bulge in right inguinal region, impulse on cough, no tenderness, transillumination negative

hidden_data.us_groin: Right direct inguinal hernia with bowel content, reducible

key_learning_points: Direct = through Hesselbach triangle (medial to epigastric vessels)

patient_responses.lump: It comes out when I cough or stand for long
patient_responses.reduction: It goes back when I lie down

feedback.correct_message: Correct! Direct inguinal hernia - well diagnosed.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a direct inguinal hernia. The cough impulse + reducibility confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Direct inguinal hernia: Elective Lichtenstein tension-free mesh repair. Open or laparoscopic approach. Most patients go home same day. Avoid heavy lifting for 4-6 weeks post-op.
